name: Pre-Funnel (Social Media Pre-Frame)
lengthClass: short-form (native content units, each with a bridge CTA into a real funnel)
buildableNote: Not a page funnel — a CONTENT framework. Native posts published ON social platforms that warm cold audiences and pre-frame the offer, each ending in a bridge CTA into a squeeze/lead-magnet funnel.

goals: Warm cold social audiences organically BEFORE they ever hit the main funnel.
Pre-frame the offer so that by the time someone clicks the bridge CTA, they already trust the Attractive Character.
Draw people in natively on each platform (described as equally important as the funnel itself).
signals: Client has (or is building) an organic social presence and wants it to feed funnels, not just collect likes.
Cold audiences bounce off the funnel — they need a relationship/pre-frame first.
Client is working a Dream 100 / organic strategy.
antiSignals: Client wants immediate paid-traffic conversions only — pair this with, but don't substitute it for, a front-end funnel.
No social presence and no willingness to publish consistently.

pageStructure.purpose: High-frequency, native, behind-the-scenes + value content that builds the Attractive Character daily.
elements: Mini Epiphany-Bridge story or quick value
Bridge CTA: link sticker / 'link in bio' to the squeeze/lead-magnet funnel

pageStructure.purpose: Longer-form story + teaching that pre-frames the core offer's big domino belief.
elements: Live teaching that demos the result
Comment/DM trigger -> auto-DM the funnel link

pageStructure.purpose: Searchable, evergreen pins/articles that pull new cold audiences in over time.
elements: Pin/article links to a lead-magnet funnel

pageStructure.purpose: Where every content unit's CTA lands — convert the warmed follower into an email lead.
elements: Curiosity squeeze headline matching the content promise
Routes into the value ladder + follow-up

copyFramework.primaryPersona: Russell Brunson — Traffic Secrets persona (The Traffic Strategist): Dream 100, Hook-Story-Offer for native content, the Funnel Hub + Shadow Funnel capture doctrine.
supportingPersonas: Russell Brunson — Expert Secrets persona (The Attractive Character / Expert): Epiphany Bridge story script, the Big Domino, false-belief patterns (vehicle/internal/external), Perfect Webinar, The Stack and Close, Hook-Story-Offer.
Jim Edwards — Copywriting Secrets persona (The Copy Mechanic): the Big Promise headline formulas, fascination/curiosity bullets, the order-bump and OTO close formulas, the 'one-sentence' hook.
scripts: Hook-Story-Offer (Traffic Secrets) as the spine of every native content piece.
Attractive Character elements + mini Epiphany Bridges (Expert Secrets) to build the relationship.
Jim Edwards scroll-stopping hooks for the first line/second of each piece.
copyFramework.notes: The platforms are not the funnel — they are the PRE-frame. Every piece must end with ONE bridge CTA into an owned squeeze/lead funnel; never leave the relationship trapped on a platform you don't own.

pages: No content pages live in GHL — the posts live on the platforms. GHL hosts the BRIDGE DESTINATION (squeeze/lead-magnet funnel).
Build the destination as a GHL Funnel: Squeeze -> Thank-you/delivery.
skill44Widgets: Skill 44 Form widget: the squeeze opt-in that every social bridge CTA links to.
Skill 44 'link in bio'-style landing (a GHL funnel/landing page) aggregating multiple offers if needed.
automations: Workflow: new lead from social bridge -> tag by source -> Soap Opera Sequence.
Optional: GHL social-planner to schedule the native content + auto-DM keyword automation for comment->DM bridges.
ghlBuild.notes: Skill 6 builds the bridge-destination funnel/landing; Skill 44 builds its opt-in form. The content templates themselves are a documented playbook (per platform), not a GHL page build. Deliverable = a content/post-template framework + the one funnel they all point to.
